reason: Overwhelming community consensus that 9PM is the go-to affordable Ultra Male alternative — shared lavender/cinnamon + heavy vanilla-amber drydown and aggressive sweet profile.
honest_differences: 9PM opens on apple rather than Ultra Male's signature pear; noticeably weaker longevity (~4-5h vs 8-12h) and projection.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica community, Fragrenza dupe roundup, multiple TikTok/YouTube 'is 9PM a clone of' comparisons all name Ultra Male as the primary reference.

reason: Shares a juicy fruity (pineapple/apple) + woody direction reviewers loosely associate with the Aventus family, but explicitly described as not a clone.
honest_differences: Rebel drives into savory caramel and cedar with no smoky birch/oakmoss chypre backbone; the Aventus link is atmospheric only.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews describe pineapple + Granny Smith apple over cedar and caramel, and note the Aventus association while stating 'this isn't a clone'.

reason: Same 9PM line and shares a sweet-fruity signature, positioned as a fruitier sibling.
honest_differences: Rebel is fruit/caramel-led and far less spicy-vanillic than the original 9PM.
supporting_evidence: Line relationship; Fragrantica note profiles differ substantially.

reason: Positioned as a spicier elixir interpretation in the Ultra Male sweet-amber lineage.
honest_differences: Only ~50% similar per reviewers; markedly spicier (pimento, nutmeg, cardamom) and darker than Ultra Male.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica user reviews: 'definitely inspired by JPG elixir absolu, but only about 50% similar.'

reason: Boozy toffee-and-fruit extrait some reviewers liken to the 'Wanted Elixir' direction.
honest_differences: Widely regarded as closer to an Afnan original; the Wanted link is contested and single-source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica/decant listings compare it to Wanted Elixir while noting many consider it an original creation.

reason: Consensus reads it as a Bleu de Chanel base fused with a YSL Y-style sweeter drydown — a genuine two-parent hybrid.
honest_differences: Not a 1:1 BdC dupe; the sweeter Y-like heart pulls it away from a straight Bleu clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + multiple YouTube/TikTok reviews ('Bleu de Chanel meets YSL Y') describe the fresh-smoky BdC opening and Y-style drydown.

reason: Second parent of the hybrid: the sweeter apple/amberwood drydown leans YSL Y.
honest_differences: Fresh-aquatic BdC-style opening is not part of Y; only the drydown converges.
supporting_evidence: Same reviewer consensus describing 9AM Dive as a BdC/Y hybrid.
to: Yves Saint Laurent Y Eau de Parfum

reason: Reviewers estimate ~90% overlap with YSL Tuxedo's aromatic-woody character.
honest_differences: Turathi Brown leans more powdery; Tuxedo is a touch more fresh-aromatic. Not called a 1:1 clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica/Parfumo commentary: 'similarity to Tuxedo is at 90%... a really good variation of Tuxedo.'

reason: Marketed/reviewed as a fresh fruity-musky take on Ex Nihilo Blue Talisman.
honest_differences: Evidence is thin (brand-adjacent social posts); needs corroboration across independent reviewers.
supporting_evidence: Afnan product page + a Facebook reviewer post naming Blue Talisman as the inspiration.

reason: Broadly recognised as a potent Nishane Hacivat clone — pineapple-tinged smoky woody with oakmoss/patchouli.
honest_differences: Some note Aventus-adjacent echoes and a slightly earthier drydown than Hacivat.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews and dupe roundups repeatedly tag it as a Hacivat clone.

reason: One of the longest-standing, most-recommended budget Aventus clones — fruity green top and smoky woody base track Aventus closely.
honest_differences: EdP concentration performs ~4-6h; some batches read slightly less 'organic' than genuine Aventus.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica, Parfumo, Basenotes threads and multiple retailers explicitly sell/describe it as an Aventus clone.

reason: Widely reviewed as tracking Aventus Absolu more than the 2010 original — pineapple/apple + white florals over oakmoss and ambergris.
honest_differences: Dials back smokiness and adds a fresh apple (Y-like) facet, so it modernises rather than exactly copies Absolu.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica top-20 placement, Palette21 and YouTube 'best clone of Aventus Absolu?' reviews.

reason: Clearly draws on Oud for Greatness' saffron-oud DNA; reviewers put it around 75-80% close.
honest_differences: Described as 'inspired by' rather than a clone; drydown diverges somewhat from Initio.
supporting_evidence: Parfumo reviews and retailer listings frame it as an Oud-for-Greatness alternative at ~75-80%.
to: Initio Parfums Prives Oud for Greatness

reason: Unmistakable Silver Mountain Water DNA — bergamot/green tea/blackcurrant over musky woods.
honest_differences: Adds its own character and is not a straight 1:1 clone of SMW.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews describe clear SMW DNA that 'goes its own way'.

reason: Chases the Ombre Leather leather-floral idea with violet leaf and spice.
honest_differences: Reviews are mixed on how close it lands; oud/rose facets pull it away from Ombre Leather. Lower confidence.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews describe it as 'chasing behind Ombre Leather' with mixed opinions.

reason: Positioned and reviewed as a Pacific Chill alternative — bright citrus-mint with a green fruity heart.
honest_differences: Afnan adds fig/dates/amberwood warmth in the base, diverging from LV's cleaner finish.
supporting_evidence: Retailer listings and a 2025 YouTube 'dupe of the year?' review name Pacific Chill as the target.

reason: Almond/praline/vanilla-ambroxan gourmand that reviewers say 'totally matches up with PDM Althair'.
honest_differences: Framed as inspiration-then-improvement rather than an exact clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews and news feature naming Althair as the reference.

reason: Closest of the Afnan range to Bleu de Chanel — a spicier, woodier, nighttime BdC.
honest_differences: Reviewers say 'closest to BdC but I wouldn't call it a clone'; darker and more woody.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews + retailer listings labelling it a Bleu de Chanel alternative.

reason: Reviewed as a strong Sauvage EDT-style pepper-lavender-ambroxan fresh aromatic.
honest_differences: Solid opening but falls short on Sauvage's ambroxan base longevity/projection; reads fresher and more linear.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews and retailer descriptions calling it a 'beast mode' Sauvage alternative.

reason: No firmly established designer inspiration. A single Fragrantica review likens it to a niche 'Encelade', which is uncorroborated. Treated as original DNA with closest-profile exposed.
honest_differences: Woody-leather-incense-rose profile is distinctive; any inspiration claim is currently single-source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo note profiles; inspiration unverified — flagged for manual review.

reason: Confirmed (user + multiple reviewers): shares Bvlgari Le Gemme Tygar's grapefruit-citrus / ambroxan / patchouli / musk DNA — the first 15-20 minutes are nearly identical.
honest_differences: Turathi Blue adds a synthetic 'blue' soapy-aquatic accent, running fresher and slightly sweeter; Tygar is drier, more mineral, with a sharper grapefruit bitterness and delicate ginger heart.
supporting_evidence: User-confirmed. EVoXShop and multiple YouTube side-by-sides ('extremely accurate clone of Bvlgari Tygar') plus Fragrantica/Parfumo profiles corroborate the Tygar DNA; three names dominate Tygar-dupe discussion (Turathi Blue, Rue Broca Theoreme, Lattafa Al Qiam Silver).

reason: Base 9AM reads as a general fresh citrus-musk aromatic with no consensus designer target (unlike its 9AM Dive flanker).
honest_differences: Inexpensive everyday freshie; no strong clone claim in the community.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile; no verified inspiration — closest DNA exposed instead.

reason: The defining budget alternative to Angels' Share — shared boozy-gourmand praline/tonka/vanilla warmth and even bottle homage.
honest_differences: Kilian carries a finer cognac accord and darker plum depth; Khamrah is rounder with a crisper apple/date facet and less cognac smoke.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica/Parfumo consensus plus numerous side-by-side blog and YouTube comparisons treat Khamrah as inspired-by (not a 1:1 clone).

reason: Same Khamrah gourmand base amplified with roasted coffee and cardamom — a coffee-forward flanker.
honest_differences: The bitter Arabic-coffee counterpoint to the praline is the main divergence from the original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + multiple Khamrah-line comparison articles/videos.

reason: Inherits Khamrah's Angels' Share lineage while pushing coffee to the front.
honest_differences: The coffee/cardamom load pulls it further from Angels' Share than the original Khamrah.
supporting_evidence: Derived from the Khamrah↔Angels' Share relationship plus line comparisons.

reason: Smoky evening amplification of Khamrah — same sweet base with added incense, tobacco and oud, and huge performance.
honest_differences: Trades Khamrah's universal appeal for a dense incense-smoke-oud character; reads more masculine/evening.
supporting_evidence: ScentClones and Khamrah buying-guide comparisons; Fragrantica.

reason: The most-cited Sauvage Elixir alternative — spicy-tobacco warmth with a sweet amber base close to the Dior.
honest_differences: Sources split between 'clone' and 'inspired by'; Asad is sweeter and slightly harsher on top than Sauvage Elixir.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews, retailer listings and dupe roundups; classified INSPIRED_BY because trusted sources disagree on 'clone'.

reason: Reviewed as an outstanding Azzaro The Most Wanted clone — lavender/mirabelle/pink pepper over cacao and bourbon vanilla. Held at INSPIRED_BY (not VERIFIED_CLONE) until a second independent source confirms.
honest_differences: Slightly more vetiver-dry in the base than the original; performance close.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica note match + reviews describing it as an 'outstanding clone of Azzaro Most Wanted'. Single strong source so far — not yet cross-validated.

reason: Described as a love-child of JPG Le Beau's sweet-coconut DNA with the dark smoky facets of Bentley for Men Intense.
honest_differences: Not a clean single-target clone; the coconut-water/salt heart is its own thing. Placeholder Ultra-Male link pending a Le Beau reference node.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica review describing the Le Beau × Bentley Intense hybrid character.

reason: Second parent: the dark smoky-spicy drydown draws on Bentley for Men Intense.
honest_differences: Only the smoky base converges; the sweet coconut opening is unrelated to Bentley.
supporting_evidence: Same Fragrantica review citing Bentley for Men Intense DNA.

reason: A deeper, more concentrated evolution of Asad — spicier saffron/pepper top with tobacco-cedar-amber base.
honest_differences: Not a clone of a specific designer; positioned as a richer Asad. Exact external inspiration unconfirmed.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Lattafa listing describing it as a concentrated, crowd-pleasing evolution of Asad.

reason: Near-perfect YSL Y EDP clone — the crisp apple/ginger opening and aromatic sage/geranium heart are effectively identical DNA.
honest_differences: Stronger lavender and a sweeter tonka drydown vs a touch less sage than Y EDP.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica note-for-note match and widespread community consensus as the best Y EDP clone.
to: Yves Saint Laurent Y Eau de Parfum

reason: Spicy-citrus + solar florals over a leathery amber base tracks the Paco Rabanne 1 Million Parfum direction.
honest_differences: Extrait richness and a more tuberose-solar heart distinguish it; called a dupe rather than exact.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer listings marketing it as a 1 Million Parfum clone/dupe.

reason: The reference Oud for Greatness clone — saffron/nutmeg/lavender over agarwood/patchouli, ~95% identical in the drydown per side-by-sides.
honest_differences: Slightly less refined mid-stage; blind-test differences mostly vanish within ~30 minutes.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica, Parfumo, Fragrance-Dupes and many retailers explicitly sell it as the Oud-for-Greatness clone.
from: Lattafa Bade'e Al Oud Oud for Glory
to: Initio Parfums Prives Oud for Greatness

reason: Reviewed as a blend of Nishane Hacivat and Nishane Ani — pineapple-fruity with a sweet vanilla/incense turn.
honest_differences: Neither parent is cloned outright; it fuses Hacivat's fruity-woody with Ani's sweet gourmand.
supporting_evidence: Retailer/community descriptions naming a Hacivat + Ani mix.
from: Lattafa Bade'e Al Oud Honor & Glory

reason: Second parent: the sweet vanilla/tonka gourmand facet leans Nishane Ani.
honest_differences: Only the sweet drydown converges with Ani; opening is fruity-woody Hacivat.
supporting_evidence: Same retailer/community sourcing for the Hacivat+Ani blend claim.
from: Lattafa Bade'e Al Oud Honor & Glory

reason: A rosy, slightly aldehydic woody-amber in the Initio Atomic Rose direction.
honest_differences: Oud underpinning gives it an Arabian twist not present in Atomic Rose; single-strand sourcing.
supporting_evidence: Community/retailer descriptions naming Atomic Rose as the inspiration.

reason: Juicy-apple fruity-floral aligned by a single retailer with Kayali Eden Juicy Apple 01.
honest_differences: Only one retailer source; needs corroboration before treating as a firm dupe.
supporting_evidence: Amazon/retailer listing describing it as a Kayali Eden Juicy Apple clone.

reason: Reviewed as cloning one of the best Acqua di Gio facets — bergamot/rosemary with sea notes over musky amber.
honest_differences: About 6h longevity; a touch more violet/patchouli than ADG EDT.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews describing it as basically ADG EDT with sea notes.

reason: ~70-80% Gris Charnel — creamy black-tea/cardamom/fig over iris and sandalwood.
honest_differences: Not an exact clone; swaps some citrus for a creamier tea and reads slightly sweeter.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews estimating 70-80% similarity to Gris Charnel.

reason: Best-regarded Bleu Electrique alternative — cardamom/ginger/citrus with lavender-geranium-mint over woody amber (~85%).
honest_differences: Moderate projection after the first hours; an Arabic twist keeps it from being exact.
supporting_evidence: YouTube reviews + Parfumo/Fragrantica describing it as the best Bleu Electrique dupe at ~85%.
to: Yves Saint Laurent La Nuit de l'Homme Bleu Electrique

reason: The Musamam base carries a woody-smoky-amber character variously likened to Tom Ford Ebène Fumé and YSL M7; Black Intense deepens it.
honest_differences: Community is split (Ebène Fumé vs M7 vs Guilty Absolute); no firm single target. Placeholder link pending an Ebène Fumé reference node.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica/Parfumo/retailer commentary noting mixed M7 / Ebène Fumé / Guilty Absolute comparisons for the Musamam line.

reason: A juicy-pineapple boost on the Aventus/Hacivat fruity-woody-smoky DNA.
honest_differences: Sweeter and more overtly pineapple than Hacivat; balsam-fir/oud base gives its own signature.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + community describing it as Aventus/Hacivat DNA with a pineapple lift.

reason: Shares the Aventus fruity-chypre pineapple/smoky-woody family, offered as an affordable alternative.
honest_differences: Less smoky birch and more overt sweetness than Aventus.
supporting_evidence: Same community consensus placing it in the Aventus/Hacivat cluster.

reason: Widely called the perfect Sedley dupe — minty-lime fresh aromatic, smooth and long-lasting for the profile.
honest_differences: A touch simpler than Sedley; excellent value ('$28 clone of a $300 fragrance').
supporting_evidence: The Sniff Test, Bloom Perfumery and multiple TikTok reviews naming Sedley as the target.

reason: No reliable designer/niche inspiration surfaced in research. Stored as original DNA with closest woody-oud profile exposed.
honest_differences: Warm woody-oud/amber; any clone claim currently unsupported.
supporting_evidence: Insufficient corroborated evidence — flagged for manual review in a later pass.

reason: Reviewed as indistinguishable-to-most from JPG Le Male Elixir's sweet vanilla-tobacco-lavender profile.
honest_differences: Conflicting evidence: other roundups instead call it a Spicebomb interpretation. Confidence held low pending cross-check.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews (Le Male Elixir) vs Fragrenza/PerfumesRoom/FridayCharm (Spicebomb) — sources disagree.
to: Jean Paul Gaultier Le Male Elixir

reason: A close, affordable Creed Aventus interpretation: citrus/apple/mint over jasmine-rose-patchouli with oakmoss-amber-cedar.
honest_differences: A step below top Aventus clones on refinement and smoke; more floral-woody than smoky-fruity.
supporting_evidence: Parfumo + The Perfumes Room reviews naming Aventus as the reference.

reason: Widely reviewed as a near-1:1 Louis Vuitton Pacific Chill clone — 'identical after a few minutes' in multiple side-by-sides.
honest_differences: LV holds ~4h vs ~3h here; some rank Azzurra Emerald Wade marginally closer.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + multiple YouTube/TikTok side-by-side reviews ('strongest Pacific Chill clone').

reason: Line-mate of Pacific Aura in the Pacific Chill citrus-aquatic lane.
honest_differences: Fragrantica dates Pacific to 2022, before Pacific Chill (2023) — timeline inconsistency; treat as inspired/adjacent pending verification.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + TikTok 'what is Rayhaan Pacific a clone of'.

reason: Reviewed as the best current Wavechild dupe — melon/watermelon/coconut summer sweetness.
honest_differences: Single-strand sourcing; niche target with limited corroboration.
supporting_evidence: Retailer + Fragrantica descriptions naming Wavechild.

reason: Clean lemon-bergamot citrus reviewers loosely compare to Dior Homme Cologne.
honest_differences: Reviews vary on closeness; comparison is soft, not a confirmed dupe.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica user reviews.

reason: Aromatic-aquatic with no consensus designer target; one reviewer likens the fleeting opening to Afnan 9AM Dive.
honest_differences: No stable clone claim; closest DNA exposed instead.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.

reason: Ultra Male-style sweet-spicy: lavender/pear/mint over cinnamon-cumin and vanilla-amber.
honest_differences: Cumin adds a skankier facet not in Ultra Male; a variation rather than a 1:1 clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + TikTok/YouTube 'Rayhaan Lion clone of' reviews.

reason: Halfeti-style spicy-milky-incense: nutmeg/clove over milk-rose with amber, patchouli, frankincense, labdanum.
honest_differences: A more affordable, slightly simpler Halfeti reading.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + fragranceknowledge review naming Halfeti.

reason: Reviewed at ~95% of Emporio Armani Stronger With You Leather — chestnut/spice over a vanilla-leather-oud base.
honest_differences: Slightly more leather; performance a notch below the original (6-8h).
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + retailer/YouTube all tag Stronger With You Leather.
to: Emporio Armani Stronger With You Leather

reason: A well-executed Creed Centaurus interpretation — cinnamon/pink pepper/cardamom over tonka-vanilla-benzoin.
honest_differences: Softer and a touch simpler than Centaurus; not aggressive.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + decant listings naming Centaurus.

reason: 'Rayhaan Panther' was not found in Fragrantica/Parfumo or any reviewed source. Product identity itself is unconfirmed — placeholder pending manual verification.
honest_differences: No scent data or inspiration available; do NOT surface in user-facing features until confirmed.
supporting_evidence: Absent from Rayhaan designer listing during research (verified names include Lion, Tiger, Wolf, Obsidian, Terra, Italia, Aquatica, Pharaoh, Divine).

supporting_evidence: Absent from Rayhaan designer listing during research (verified names include Lion, Tiger, Wolf, Obsidian, Terra, Italia, Aquatica, Pharaoh, Divine).

reason: The reference budget alternative to the Paco Rabanne Invictus aquatic-fruity accord, with added ambergris for stronger longevity.
honest_differences: NOT an Aventus clone (a common myth) — different family; also carries some Acqua di Gio Absolu freshness.
supporting_evidence: Multiple guides (Qamare, Mahadi, Sense Perfume) confirm the Invictus/Invictus-Aqua lineage and debunk the Aventus label.

reason: A sweeter, green-apple/bubblegum Invictus Aqua-style flanker of Hawas.
honest_differences: More candied than Invictus Aqua; reads younger/sweeter.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews naming Invictus Aqua.

reason: Same Hawas aquatic-fruity backbone with a sweeter, icier twist.
honest_differences: Flanker relationship; Ice is candied where the original is more marine.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica line comparisons.

reason: Reviewed as a Nishane Hacivat dupe — fresh fruity opening over a smooth woody-amber base.
honest_differences: A grounded, slightly cheaper-smelling Hacivat; less oakmoss lift.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + YouTube 'Rasasi Hawas Black - Nishane Hacivat Dupe'.

reason: Reviewed as a Gissah Imperial Valley take — clary sage and fruity jam into a marine white-floral heart.
honest_differences: Niche target with limited corroboration; single main source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.

reason: Hawas For Her Éclat reads as a PdM Delina / Delina Exclusif lychee-rose interpretation.
honest_differences: Pear is less dominant and the drydown sweeter than Delina Exclusif — not a 1:1.
supporting_evidence: Parfumo + Fragrantica + retailer listings naming Delina.

reason: A greener aquatic reviewed as an Invictus Parfum-adjacent take within the Hawas family.
honest_differences: Comparisons vary; the green twist pulls it away from a clean dupe.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.

reason: Bergamot/ginger/apple spicy-woody reviewers say recalls YSL MYSLF L'Absolu.
honest_differences: Soft comparison; base is thinner/woodier than MYSLF.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + iFragrance reviews.

reason: Praline/chocolate/vanilla gourmand reviewers place in the PdM Althair lane.
honest_differences: Late-to-market Althair-type; corroboration still thin.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.

reason: Juicy yellow-fruit and tropical-fruit profile reviewed as a Xerjoff Erba Pura take.
honest_differences: Newer release; single main source so far.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + ScentAdvice/iFragrance naming Erba Pura.

reason: No confirmed designer/niche inspiration: richer Hawas, no distinct external target. Stored as original DNA; scent DNA where available exposes closest profile.
honest_differences: Inspiration unverified or target house unconfirmed — flagged for a later deepening pass.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile / retailer listings; no cross-validated clone target.

reason: Long-standing, widely-recommended Tom Ford Tuscan Leather alternative — raspberry/saffron/smoke over a suede-leather base.
honest_differences: Emphasises smoke and raspberry a touch more than leather vs the original; also compared to LNDH by some.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + Basenotes consensus as a strong Tuscan Leather dupe.

reason: Regarded as essentially a 1:1 Memo Irish Leather clone — leather, juniper and mate — despite the misleading name.
honest_differences: Contains no real ambergris focus; name is marketing.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + Basenotes threads calling it an Irish Leather clone.

reason: Reviewed as 'La Yuqawam Homme without the raspberry' — same leathery-oud Tuscan Leather relative.
honest_differences: Drops the raspberry brightness; darker and more oud-leather.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo comparisons within the La Yuqawam line.

reason: Shares the Tuscan Leather leather-suede DNA via the La Yuqawam base, minus the raspberry.
honest_differences: Further from Tuscan Leather than the original La Yuqawam Homme.
supporting_evidence: Derived from La Yuqawam line reviews.

reason: A good Valentino V pour Homme alternative — cumin/cardamom over rose-orris with a vanilla-tonka-amber base.
honest_differences: Cheaper materials; a touch more powdery-spicy.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + YouTube naming V pour Homme.

reason: Terre d'Hermès-inspired grapefruit/pepper over vetiver-cedar-patchouli with an oakmoss-benzoin-amber base.
honest_differences: Built differently — more oakmoss/benzoin; shares the DNA rather than cloning it.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + Scent Buddy review noting the Terre d'Hermès inspiration.

reason: A rosier Creed Silver Mountain Water — bergamot/geranium/lavender over rose-sandalwood with a musk-oakmoss-oud base.
honest_differences: Reaches SMW's inky freshness by a rosier route; not a straight copy.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + retailer ('Creed SMW clone') listings.

reason: A distinct green-woody companion to Al Wisam Day with no clear external designer target.
honest_differences: Reviewers describe it as its own thing, unlike the SMW-leaning Day version.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews.

reason: Distinctive tomato-leaf/rose woody-leather with no consensus designer target; sits near the Silver Mountain Water cluster but is largely original.
honest_differences: Green-sour rose facet is unusual; no reliable single clone target.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Basenotes reviews noting its uniqueness.

reason: 'Rasasi Yanis 12:01 AM' not found with a note pyramid or inspiration in reviewed sources — placeholder pending scent data.
honest_differences: No scent data available; do NOT surface in user-facing features until researched.
supporting_evidence: Absent from indexed Rasasi profiles during research.

reason: Marketed as inspired by Kilian Angels' Share — a boozy-praline-vanilla gourmand.
honest_differences: Reviewers say it lands closer to Lattafa Khamrah than to Angels' Share itself; cardamom/fig-leaf opening differs from the cognac of the Kilian.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews naming Angels' Share while noting the Khamrah resemblance.

reason: Community consensus places it in the same boozy-spiced-praline gourmand cluster as (and closer to) Lattafa Khamrah.
honest_differences: Aariz Intense is a touch woodier/lactonic; both share the Angels' Share lineage.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews explicitly comparing it to Khamrah.

reason: Reviewed as close to Gris Charnel (Extrait) — cardamom/fig with a creamy tea-woody, leathery drydown.
honest_differences: Al Noor adds more overt leather/labdanum; single main review source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica review ('pretty close to gris charnel extrait').

reason: Reviewed/retailer-described as inspired by LV Imagination — citrus over ginger-cinnamon-neroli with a black-tea/ambroxan/guaiac base.
honest_differences: A more affordable, slightly less airy Imagination.
supporting_evidence: Discovery Decants + Parfumo + Fragrantica naming Imagination.

reason: Reviewers find the drydown so close to Armani Sì Intense it's hard to tell apart — blackcurrant-fruity chypre with patchouli.
honest_differences: Opening is fruitier (grapes); the Sì resemblance is strongest in the drydown.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming Armani Sì Intense.

reason: Reviewed at ~85-90% of Dior Sauvage EDP/Parfum — a fresh-spicy ambroxan aromatic.
honest_differences: A hair less refined and slightly shorter than genuine Sauvage EDP.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer descriptions naming Sauvage.

reason: Carries the unmistakable L'Eau d'Issey pour Homme DNA (calone/spice) in a bluer, fresher reading.
honest_differences: Bluer/sweeter than the original Issey; a modern reinterpretation.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming L'Eau d'Issey pour Homme.
to: Issey Miyake L'Eau d'Issey pour Homme

reason: Opens very close to Valentino Uomo Born in Roma Intense with a slight Sauvage Elixir facet.
honest_differences: A two-way comparison (Valentino + Sauvage Elixir) — not a clean single-target clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.
to: Valentino Uomo Born in Roma Intense

reason: Often touted as an Aventus clone; in practice a sweeter, ambery, oud-tinged cousin rather than a close match.
honest_differences: Reviewers agree it is 'related but not that close' — vanilla/ambroxan sweetness and oud pull it away from Aventus.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews + YouTube ('touted as Aventus clone... not that close').

reason: One reviewer calls it the best dupe for the Angels' Share (Paradis) opening, just creamier.
honest_differences: Saffron/clove and akigalawood give it an oud-gourmand twist distinct from the Kilian; single main source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica review.

reason: Reviewed as a brighter, red-fruit take on Tom Ford Vanilla Sex — sweet raspberry-lemon into a vanilla-benzoin base.
honest_differences: The strawberry/raspberry lift makes it fruitier than Vanilla Sex; not a 1:1.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews + YouTube clone comparison.

reason: A grapefruit-forward twist on the Bvlgari Tygar citrus DNA — reviewers note the grapefruit reads more realistic than many Tygar alternatives.
honest_differences: Simpler and more citrus-linear than Tygar; shares DNA rather than cloning it.

reason: Marketed and reviewed as a Bvlgari Le Gemme Onekh-style smoky oud — olibanum/cedar over oud, leather and caramel.
honest_differences: A sweeter caramel facet vs the drier original; retailer-led sourcing.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer (Aromatick) listing it as an Onekh clone.

reason: Reviewers call it ~98% YSL Tuxedo — violet-leaf/bergamot over cedar-rose with a patchouli-oud-amber-vanilla base.
honest_differences: Single strong review; slightly more oud in the base than Tuxedo.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming Tuxedo at ~98%.

reason: Reviewed as a fresher, less-sweet take on Dior Sauvage Elixir.
honest_differences: Fragrantica's published pyramid (red fruits/lychee/gourmand) doesn't match Sauvage Elixir's spicy profile — a source discrepancy; treat with caution.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica user reviews ('clone of Sauvage Elixir, fresher/unsweetened').

reason: Marketed as a blend of Creed Aventus and Baccarat Rouge 540 — fruity blackcurrant/bergamot with a sweet ambery base.
honest_differences: Neither parent is cloned outright; it fuses Aventus fruitiness with BR540 sweetness.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer descriptions naming the Aventus x BR540 blend.

reason: Second parent: the sweet amberwood-saffron facet leans Baccarat Rouge 540.
honest_differences: Only the sweet ambery drydown converges with BR540.
supporting_evidence: Same Aventus x BR540 marketing/review sourcing.

reason: A more natural, wearable Xerjoff Erba Pura take — tropical/green fruity-citrus over amber-musk.

supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews naming Erba Pura.

reason: Juicy-fruity placed near the Xerjoff Erba (Pura/'Gold') family by reviewers.

reason: Widely regarded as the closest, most accurate Angels' Share clone (~85%) — boozy cognac/cinnamon/oak over praline and tonka.
honest_differences: A slightly synthetic boozy edge the original avoids; ~6-7h vs the Kilian's longer wear.
supporting_evidence: Scentonym (85% match) + Parfumo + YouTube all label it an Angels' Share clone.

reason: A fresher dry-wood-and-cinnamon flanker of Royal Blend, in the same Angels' Share gourmand lane.
honest_differences: Fresher and less boozy than the original Royal Blend.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica line reviews.

reason: Reviewed as inspired by Angels' Share Paradis' middle notes, led by a strong raspberry/liquor accord.
honest_differences: The raspberry lead pulls it toward a fruity gourmand rather than a straight Angels' Share clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews referencing Angels' Share Paradis.

reason: Reviewed as a near-1:1 Parfums de Marly Althair clone — almond/praline/vanilla-ambroxan gourmand.
honest_differences: Single detailed source so far; held below VERIFIED_CLONE pending a second.
supporting_evidence: Scentonym-style review calling it a very accurate Althair clone.

reason: A richer limited-edition flanker of Liquid Brun in the Althair gourmand lane.
honest_differences: Detailed pyramid not documented; treated as a stronger Liquid Brun.
supporting_evidence: Retailer listings within the Liquid Brun line.
from: French Avenue Liquid Brun Limited Edition

reason: Reviewed as effectively identical to Parfums de Marly Greenly — green apple/bergamot over cedar-cashmeran-violet.
honest_differences: Wearer blind-tests found them the same; a couple of sources, mostly enthusiast.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + FragranceLovers naming Greenly as the dupe target.

reason: Coconut/watermelon/citrus with a cacao-ambergris base — a Wavechild-leaning fruity aquatic.
honest_differences: Comparison inferred from the note profile; not a widely-cited dupe claim.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile; note-based inference.

reason: A well-known Nishane Ani clone — ginger/cardamom over pink pepper-rose with a sweet vanilla-cedar base.
honest_differences: A touch simpler/sweeter than Ani.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Fragrenza roundup naming Ani.

reason: Rum/coffee/sugarcane profile reviewed as a Kilian Black Phantom clone.
honest_differences: Slightly boozier-sweet than Black Phantom.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer (My Smell Shop) + TikTok naming Black Phantom.

reason: Recorded on the 'Pinnace' reading — a bitter-lemon Louis Vuitton Pacific Chill clone.
honest_differences: NAME AMBIGUITY: 'Spectre Pinnace' is not a confirmed single release; likely conflates FA 'Spectre' (leather) and 'Pinnace' (Pacific Chill clone). Needs confirmation.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica 'Pinnace' profile ('clone of Pacific Chill').

reason: A wearable, elegant take on the Hugo Boss Bottled apple-cinnamon DNA.
honest_differences: More rounded/vanillic than the sharper original Bottled.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming Boss Bottled DNA.

reason: Widely recognised as an affordable Louis Vuitton Afternoon Swim alternative — mandarin/orange/bergamot over ginger and ambergris.
honest_differences: A touch less radiant/expensive-smelling than the LV.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo naming Afternoon Swim.

reason: Reviewed as a ~96% Maison Crivelli Oud Maracuja clone — passionfruit/saffron over oud with a leather-guaiac-amber base.
honest_differences: A hair less refined than the niche original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer (Rimal Al Misk, My Smell Shop) naming Oud Maracuja.

reason: Cinnamon/saffron/cardamom over dates-caramel-oud-incense — a Maison Crivelli Oud Cadenza clone.
honest_differences: Sweeter/more caramelic than the original; single main source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + YouTube reviews naming Oud Cadenza.

reason: Boozy whiskey aromatic (cinnamon/basil over bourbon whiskey and lavender); no confirmed designer target.
honest_differences: Original-leaning; any clone claim currently unsupported.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile.

reason: Reviewed as a clone of 'Old Fashioned' (some say Single Malt), but the target house is unconfirmed — a boozy oriental fougere.
honest_differences: Named target could not be pinned to a confirmed release; flagged.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.

reason: Creamy sweet gourmand with no confirmed designer target.
honest_differences: Original-leaning gourmand.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile.

reason: NAME AMBIGUITY: appears to conflate FA 'Meringue' (a Crème de Cuir-leaning woody-floral-musk) and 'Luscious' (a pistachio gourmand compared to Lattafa Éclaire Pistache). No single confirmed product/target.
honest_differences: Recorded on the pistachio-gourmand reading; product identity needs confirmation.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica 'Meringue' and 'Luscious' profiles.

reason: A black-tea-heavy Louis Vuitton Imagination interpretation — citrus/ginger/neroli over black tea, ambroxan and guaiac.
honest_differences: Heavier on black tea/incense than Imagination; sits among several Imagination clones.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews placing it in the Imagination clone cluster.

reason: Regarded as the best-performing Parfums de Marly Layton Exclusif alternative at a fraction of the price.
honest_differences: Critics note the dupes don't fully capture Layton Exclusif's animalic depth.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + decant listings naming Layton Exclusif.

reason: A more oriental, sensual interpretation of Penhaligon's The Bewitching Yasmine — jasmine sambac with cardamom-coffee and oud.
honest_differences: Adds oud/coffee darkness the original doesn't have; interpretation rather than clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming The Bewitching Yasmine.

reason: A Penhaligon's Sartorial clone — aldehydic/metallic opening with beeswax, lavender and leather.
honest_differences: A touch less refined than Sartorial's beeswax accord.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo naming Sartorial.

reason: Smoky leather profile reviewers find close to Tom Ford Ombre Leather.
honest_differences: Slightly sweeter/oudier drydown than Ombre Leather; some cite other leathers too.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming Ombre Leather.

reason: A boozy-berry Stephane Humbert Lucas Pink Boa dupe — blackberry/raspberry/vodka over a fruity musky base.
honest_differences: Niche target; a touch simpler than the SHL original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica review feature naming SHL Pink Boa.

reason: Inspired by Xerjoff Deified (Tony Iommi) — saffron/apple/cinnamon over a leather-rose-papyrus base; strong performance.
honest_differences: Very similar per reviewers; a hair less refined than the Xerjoff.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Bloom Perfumery naming Deified.

reason: Reviewed as the best Dior Sauvage EDT dupe — bergamot over pepper-lavender and an ambroxan-vetiver base (the ambroxan-bomb era).
honest_differences: Slightly less cold/peppery than the original Sauvage.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Discovery Decants naming Sauvage EDT.

reason: Reviewed as a 1:1 Emporio Armani Stronger With You Sandalwood clone — saffron/chestnut over a creamy sandalwood-vanilla base.
honest_differences: Reported as stronger-projecting than the original; single main source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo naming SWY Sandalwood.
to: Emporio Armani Stronger With You Sandalwood

reason: A Dior Tobacolor clone — chocolate/mandarin over honey-patchouli-caramel with a tolu-tonka base.
honest_differences: Sweeter/more chocolatey than the original; single main source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer listings naming Tobacolor.

reason: A favoured Bleu de Chanel (L'Exclusif) dupe — grapefruit/mint over ginger-nutmeg with an incense-sandalwood base.
honest_differences: Community places it among the top BdC clones; a hair less refined than the Chanel.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews naming Bleu de Chanel.

reason: Inspired by Stephane Humbert Lucas Venom Incarnat — strawberry/caramel over a leather-patchouli-tonka base.
honest_differences: Niche target; a touch more gourmand than the SHL original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + ScentAdvice naming SHL Venom Incarnat.

reason: Green-tea/fig woody with no confirmed designer target.
honest_differences: Original-leaning matcha woody.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo profiles.

reason: Fruity-praline chypre reviewers link to a niche 'God of Fire', but that target house could not be confirmed.
honest_differences: Named target unverifiable; stored as original DNA with closest profile.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews.

reason: Boozy whiskey-spiced woody with no confirmed designer target.
honest_differences: Original-leaning boozy woody.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile.

reason: Zenith-line sweet vanilla; pyramid and any target not documented.
honest_differences: Awaiting a documented note pyramid.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica designer listing.

reason: Smoky-apple-tobacco amber with no confirmed designer target.
honest_differences: Original-leaning smoky amber.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile.

reason: Creamy coconut-white-floral with no confirmed designer target.
honest_differences: Original-leaning tropical floral.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profile.

reason: Reads as a YSL MYSLF interpretation — orange-blossom floral-woody amber with less of the bright citrus opening.
honest_differences: Tones down MYSLF's citrus lift; more myrrh/oakmoss depth.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming MYSLF.

reason: A candied take reviewers link to Oud Maracuja (and SHL Cuir InfraRouge) — raspberry/cotton-candy over a leather-oud base.
honest_differences: One reviewer stresses it's an original standalone that merely resembles those; not a clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica reviews naming Oud Maracuja / Cuir InfraRouge.

reason: No cross-validated designer/niche inspiration: Fruity oriental floral with no confirmed designer target. Stored as original DNA with closest profile where available.
honest_differences: Inspiration unverified — flagged for a later deepening pass.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profiles; no confirmed clone target.

supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profiles; no confirmed clone target.

honest_differences: Inspiration unverified — flagged for a later deepening pass.

honest_differences: Inspiration unverified — flagged for a later deepening pass.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica profiles; no confirmed clone target.

reason: The single most recommended Creed Aventus clone in the hobby — same pineapple-smoke-birch-oakmoss DNA, often out-projecting and out-lasting the original.
honest_differences: Sharper, more synthetic 'Lemon Pledge' opening than Aventus' smoother start.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Basenotes + DecantPlanet + multiple guides universally sell/describe it as the Aventus clone.
from: Armaf Club de Nuit Intense Man EDT

reason: The EDP concentration of the canonical Aventus clone — smoother, deeper and smokier than the EDT.
honest_differences: Leans muskier/smokier with a Middle-Eastern edge vs the fresher genuine Aventus.
supporting_evidence: Vivir + My Scent Stories EDT/EDP/Parfum comparisons; Fragrantica.
from: Armaf Club de Nuit Intense Man EDP

reason: Parfum-concentration Aventus clone that refines the pineapple-smoke-amber balance for a denser, smoother drydown — often called the best of the three.
honest_differences: Tones down the harsh lemon; richer and juicier than EDT/EDP.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica (Limited Edition Parfum) + Aromatick + Vivir naming Aventus.
from: Armaf Club de Nuit Intense Man Limited Edition

reason: Same Club de Nuit Intense DNA at higher concentration — smoother and smokier than the EDT.
honest_differences: EDP trades the EDT's citrus sharpness for depth.
supporting_evidence: Vivir/My Scent Stories line comparisons.
from: Armaf Club de Nuit Intense Man EDP
to: Armaf Club de Nuit Intense Man EDT

reason: An Aventus Absolu interpretation — pineapple/pepper/caramel over a mossy white-wood heart with gourmand sweetness and a Hacivat-style facet.
honest_differences: Reviewers stress it's an interpretation, not a clone: sweeter and peppery, less fresh than Aventus.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Scent Grail + Parfumo naming Aventus Absolu.

reason: A Creed Silver Mountain Water clone — bergamot/metallic-lemon/blackcurrant over an ambroxan-musk base — that out-projects the original.
honest_differences: A touch more metallic/tart than SMW; stronger performance.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + Basenotes naming Silver Mountain Water.

reason: Pretty much identical to Creed Millésime Impérial — a watery-fruity woody-floral-musk, stronger and fruitier than the original.
honest_differences: Creed MI is lighter/airier; Milestone is denser and more fruit-forward.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + Basenotes naming Millésime Impérial.

reason: An ~85-90% Baccarat Rouge 540 clone — same saffron/jasmine → amberwood/ambergris → cedar/fir-resin structure; hard to tell apart in the air.
honest_differences: A hair less refined/expensive-smelling than the MFK original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + iFragrance ('best BR540 clone') + retailer listings.

reason: Most commonly compared to Dior Sauvage Elixir — a mature spicy-sweet woody.
honest_differences: Multiple targets cited (also Tom Ford Noir de Noir and LNDH); not a clean single-target clone.
supporting_evidence: Perfume Nez + Parfumo + PerfumenCologne discussing the Sauvage Elixir / Noir de Noir / LNDH comparisons.
from: Armaf Club de Nuit Urban Man Elixir

reason: A Bleu de Chanel clone with amped-up ginger/spice and stronger performance.
honest_differences: More overtly gingery/spicy than BdC.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + YouTube naming Bleu de Chanel.

reason: A brighter, gingery Bleu de Chanel clone — EDT-like opening, EDP-like heart/base; ~65-75% overall.
honest_differences: Ginger and citrus are amped up; the full drydown converges more than the opening.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + fragplace comparison + iFragrance naming Bleu de Chanel.
from: Armaf Club de Nuit Blue Iconic

reason: A JPG Scandal Pour Homme clone — mandarin/saffron/sage over caramel-tonka with an ambroxan-vetiver base.
honest_differences: A touch sweeter/cheaper-smelling than the original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Jomashop 'Armaf Odyssey designer dupes' + retailer naming Scandal Pour Homme.
to: Jean Paul Gaultier Scandal Pour Homme

reason: An overt YSL Y EDP clone — apple/ginger-adjacent citrus over sage-geranium with a tonka-cedar base.
honest_differences: A citrus-aromatic fougère reading of Y; slightly simpler.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Jomashop Odyssey dupes guide naming YSL Y EDP.
to: Yves Saint Laurent Y Eau de Parfum

reason: Reviewed as a Paco Rabanne Invictus Platinum clone — a fresher aquatic Odyssey.
honest_differences: Note pyramid thinly documented; single main source.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Jomashop Odyssey dupes guide naming Invictus Platinum.

reason: Cardamom/neroli/orange-blossom amber that reviewers open-compare to Tom Ford Noir Extreme (with Spicebomb Extreme facets).
honest_differences: Multiple comparisons cited; more of an 'in the vein of' than a 1:1 clone.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer (Rimal Al Misk) + YouTube discussing Noir Extreme / Spicebomb Extreme.

reason: A Bleu de Chanel-leaning fresh woody (a lavender-based, non-smoky BdC reading); some reviewers also cite Dior Sauvage.
honest_differences: Two-way comparison (BdC / Sauvage); not a clean single target.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo reviews naming Bleu de Chanel and Sauvage.

reason: Reviewers read it as a Sauvage × Invictus mixture — bright citrus with an ambroxan-woody freshness.
honest_differences: Not a single-target clone; overlaps CDN Urban Man Elixir and Ventana in the Sauvage space.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Basenotes discussion describing a Sauvage/Invictus mix.

reason: Second parent: the aquatic-fresh facet leans Paco Rabanne Invictus.
honest_differences: Only the fresh aquatic side converges with Invictus.
supporting_evidence: Same Sauvage/Invictus-mix community sourcing.

reason: Inspired by Mancera Red Tobacco — spicy incense/saffron/green-apple over a tobacco-guaiac-amber base.
honest_differences: Not a 1:1 clone; a strong fragrance in its own right per reviewers.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + retailer (Shop with Hustle) naming Mancera Red Tobacco.

reason: A Dior Sauvage clone with near-identical performance per reviewers — fresh-spicy pepper/bergamot over ambroxan-woods.
honest_differences: Note pyramid thinly documented; sits alongside Armaf's other Sauvage takes.
supporting_evidence: Basenotes + Fragrantica community naming Sauvage.

reason: An ~95%, near-1:1 Paco Rabanne Invictus Victory Elixir clone — lavender/cardamom/pepper over a vanilla-tonka-incense base, basically indistinguishable in the drydown.
honest_differences: Also matches Zimaya Al Embratur Elixir; a hair less refined than the original.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Parfumo + retailer listings naming Invictus Victory Elixir.

reason: A close Versace Eau Fraiche clone — star-fruit/lemon/bergamot over sage-pepper with a woody-saffron base (with Dylan Blue / Sauvage-freshness facets).
honest_differences: A budget reading of Eau Fraiche; moderate longevity.
supporting_evidence: Fragrantica + Vivir + retailer naming Versace Eau Fraiche.
